response: Python was developed by Guido van Rossum, a Dutch programmer. He began working on the language as a hobby project in 1989 at the Centrum Wiskunde & Informatica (CWI), or the National Research Institute for Mathematics and Computer Science, in the Netherlands. The first version of Python was released in 1991. Van Rossum served as the "benevolent dictator for life" (BDFL) for Python, guiding its development, until he stepped down from the position in July 2018.

C++ was developed by Bjarne Stroustrup at Bell Labs (Bell Laboratories), starting in 1979. It was created as an extension to the C language and was originally known as "C with Classes" before being renamed C++ in the early 1980s.

prompt: who is the CEO of OpenAI
response: The CEO of OpenAI is Sam Altman. He co-founded OpenAI in 2015 and became its CEO in 2019.
